content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Aligned failure reconciliation and retry resume handling across the milestone runtime. Failure reconciliation now keeps controller task identity pinned to the bead-backed project while still recording daemon task IDs in failure details/journal metadata, blocked retry resume can legally re-enter `Running` or `Reconciling`, and the manual CLI terminal-failure sync path now routes through the shared attempt-aware failure reconciler so repeated `run start`/`run resume` failures escalate to `NeedsOperator` only after `MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES`. I also updated the affected regression fixtures and added coverage for blocked-retry resume transitions and CLI-side retry escalation.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Updated `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs` so controller transitions use `project_id` rather than daemon task IDs, added a synchronous helper for shared CLI/runtime reuse, and kept daemon task IDs in failure outcome details and journal metadata.
2. [Completed] Extended `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs` to allow blocked retry resumes to transition back into `Running` or `Reconciling`, with focused resume-controller regression coverage for both paths.
3. [Completed] Rerouted failed CLI terminal milestone sync in `src/cli/run.rs` through the shared failure reconciler and updated `src/cli/milestone.rs` to derive failed `milestone run` results from the post-sync controller state instead of mapping all failed snapshots straight to `NeedsOperator`.
4. [Completed] Adjusted daemon/CLI test fixtures and added regression coverage for CLI-side retry escalation and the project-backed controller identity expected by daemon failure reconciliation.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- Targeted regressions passed: `cargo test failure_reconciliation -- --nocapture`, `cargo test resume_blocked_retry_running_task_transitions_back_to_running -- --nocapture`, `cargo test resume_blocked_retry_succeeded_task_transitions_to_reconciling -- --nocapture`, `cargo test sync_terminal_milestone_task_escalates_failed_retries_after_max_retries -- --nocapture`, `cargo test prepare_milestone_controller_for_execution_initializes_claimed_state -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test sync_terminal_milestone_task_leaves_failed_runs_paused_for_resume -- --nocapture`.
- `cargo test` passed on the full suite after the remediation updates.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix build` was re-run after the fixes and advanced into `/nix/store/ypvb870dwm5428gq1c188ca9zi7lcj52-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, then into the release musl `cargo test` / `ralph-burning-stable-conformance conformance run` stage, but no final exit status was observed in-session.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` was launched on the updated tree and progressed into the release musl `ralph-burning-stable-conformance` stage, but it did not return a final success/failure exit within this session, so the authoritative Nix gate remains unresolved.
- Pre-existing live orchestration-state edits under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni854-1/` were left untouched and remain out of scope.
